scores.correctness_notes: The response provides detailed information about v4 of the library, but the gold standard references v1 (charmlibs-interfaces-tls-certificates). The events described (certificate_available, secret_expired handling, etc.) partially align but the gold standard emphasizes the interface spec (CSRs, revocation, error codes like ip_not_allowed 101) which is largely absent. The response describes implementation details of a different library version.
scores.hallucination_notes: The response describes a specific v4 library with detailed APIs (TLSCertificatesRequiresV4, regenerate_private_key, renew_certificate, renewal_relative_time parameter). These may exist in v4 but cannot be verified against the gold standard which focuses on v1/charmlibs. The level of detail about internal handlers and Juju secret integration goes beyond what can be confirmed, making these potentially hallucinated specifics.
